rationale: The predicate det_parse_end_point_equal(r1, r2) := (r1.id == r2.id) compares two Vec<u8> using Verus spec equality (==). Vec<T> in this Verus version (0.2026.04.03) has no #[verifier::ext_equal] attribute and no broadcast axiom relating Vec equality to view equality (verified by inspecting vstd/std_specs/vec.rs and vstd/view.rs where view() for Vec is uninterp). Consequently r1.id == r2.id is NOT derivable from r1.id@ == r2.id@. Even when r1@ == parse_arg_as_end_point(arg@) and r2@ == parse_arg_as_end_point(arg@) (which pins r1.id@ == r2.id@ == arg@), the SMT solver cannot conclude r1.id == r2.id for two opaque Vec values. Tried: (1) =~= on Vec, (2) =~~= (deep ext-equal), (3) broadcast use group_vec_axioms, (4) lemma_vec_obeys_view_eq / lemma_vec_obeys_deep_eq (only relate eq_spec to view, not ==), (5) #[verifier::ext_equal] on EndPoint (doesn't help because the inner Vec is still opaque), (6) explicit view chain. Minimal reproduction: requires v1@ == v2@ ensures v1 == v2 fails on bare
